text: The best way to predict the future is to create it.
author: Peter Drucker

text: Amateurs sit and wait for inspiration; the rest of us just get up and go to work.
author: Stephen King

text: Sales are contingent upon the attitude of the salesman, not the attitude of the prospect.
author: William Clement Stone

text: In the middle of difficulty lies opportunity.
author: Albert Einstein

text: Quality is remembered long after the price is forgotten.
author: Aldo Gucci

text: A goal is not always meant to be reached; it often serves simply as something to aim at.
author: Bruce Lee

text: People don't buy what you do; they buy why you do it.
author: Simon Sinek

text: If you are not taking care of your customer, your competitor will.
author: Bob Hooey

text: Don't watch the clock; do what it does. Keep going.
author: Sam Levenson

text: Every sale has five basic obstacles: no need, no money, no hurry, no desire, no trust.
author: Zig Ziglar

text: Great things in business are never done by one person; they're done by a team.
author: Steve Jobs

text: Efforts and courage are not enough without purpose and direction.
author: John F. Kennedy

text: The perfect deal waits for no one — do not wait for it either.
A pipeline forgives no procrastination.
The pessimist sees difficulty in every opportunity; the optimist sees opportunity in every difficulty.
author: Winston Churchill

text: You miss 100% of the deals you don't follow up on.
Discipline is choosing between what you want now and what you want most.
author: Abraham Lincoln

text: Trust is built in drops and lost in buckets.
The pipeline doesn't lie — but it will exaggerate a little if you let it.
Slow is smooth, and smooth is fast.
Nothing is particularly hard if you divide it into small jobs.
author: Henry Ford

text: Whether you think you can, or you think you can't, you're right.
author: Henry Ford

text: The secret of getting ahead is getting started.
author: Mark Twain

text: Show up, do the work, repeat — momentum is a compounding asset.
A prospect's silence is not a no; it's an unanswered question.
Excellence is not an act, but a habit.
author: Aristotle

text: The customer's perception is your reality.
author: Kate Zabriskie

text: Well done is better than well said.
author: Benjamin Franklin

text: Momentum solves most problems, and quietly hides the rest — for now.
Every closed-lost deal is tuition, not a loss.
Focus on the few things that actually move the pipeline.
Well-run deals are boring on purpose.
